YoY CPI bottomed for the year at 3% in June and is heading back up.  

It should be about 3.8% when the August stats come out next week.

No more rate hikes. Unless something goes wrong with inflation...

The Fed Funds rate futures are putting the largest probability on the Fed Funds rate to stay in the 5.25% to 5.50% range until the end of the year.

The default rate on credit card loans from small banks just hit a record high of 7.5%.

This is well above the 2001 high of 6.0% and 2008 high of 5.6%.

Meanwhile, these same small lenders have exposure to 70% of commercial real estate loans in the US.

The most incredible part about housing affordability?

How much worse it is outside of the US.

In New Zealand, it costs 250% more to buy than rent.

In Canada, it costs 230% more to buy than rent.

In Australia, it costs 150% more to buy than rent.

In the UK

Saudi Arabia is the latest country to offload a massive amount of their US Treasury holdings.

In February 2020, their holdings peaked at $184 billion and are now down 41%, to $108 billion.

Mortgage payment to income ratios are now pushing as high as 40%.

This is above the 2008 peak of ~34% and by far the highest level in history.

It is now nearly ~1.7x more expensive to buy a home than to rent.

A whale is buying $ARB!

The whale spent 2,000 $ETH ($3.27M) to buy 3.64M $ARB at an average price of $0.9 since Sept 3.

The whale spent 800 $ETH($1.47M) to buy 1.27M $ARB on Aug 13 but soon dumped on #Binance at a loss of ~$36K on Aug 16.
